nodes.name: scipy.integrate
nodes.description: Numerical integration and ODE solving primitives backed by SciPy.

nodes.name: quad
nodes.description: Adaptive quadrature over a scalar-valued function on a finite interval.

inputs.name: complex_func
inputs.constraints: whether the integrand returns complex values

outputs.name: quad_result
outputs.type_desc: tuple[float, float] | tuple[float, float, dict[str, object]] | tuple[float, float, dict[str, object], str]
outputs.constraints: integral estimate with absolute error and optional diagnostics

nodes.type_signature: (func: callable, a: float, b: float, complex_func: bool) -> tuple[float, float] | tuple[float, float, dict[str, object]] | tuple[float, float, dict[str, object], str]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Compute a one-dimensional definite integral with adaptive quadrature.

nodes.name: solve_ivp
nodes.description: Solve an initial value problem for an ODE system over a time span.

inputs.name: dense_output
inputs.constraints: whether to build a dense solution

nodes.type_signature: (fun: callable, t_span: tuple[float, float], y0: np.ndarray | list | tuple, method: str, t_eval: np.ndarray | list | tuple | None, dense_output: bool, events: callable | sequence | None, vectorized: bool, args: tuple[object, ...] | None, options: dict[str, object]) -> OdeResult
nodes.conceptual_summary: Integrate an ODE initial value problem.

nodes.name: simpson
nodes.description: Composite Simpson integration along one array axis.

inputs.name: dx
inputs.constraints: spacing used when x is omitted

nodes.type_signature: (y: np.ndarray | list | tuple, x: np.ndarray | list | tuple | None, dx: float, axis: int) -> float | np.ndarray
nodes.conceptual_summary: Integrate sampled data with composite Simpson's rule.
